{{Infobox event
|name=Alice saves the gardeners from beheading by concealing them inside a flower pot, outwitting the Queen's soldiers
|participants=Alice, Queen
|chapitre=8
}}

== Course of events ==

During the Queen's croquet game, Alice intervenes when the gardeners face execution at the Queen's command. By concealing the gardeners inside a flower pot, Alice successfully outwits the Queen's soldiers dispatched to carry out the sentence. This decisive action reflects the courage Alice has already demonstrated through her refusal to bow to the Queen's procession.

The intervention occurs amid circumstances that have shaped Alice's perspective: she has observed and discussed the Queen's arbitrary cruelty with the Cheshire Cat. Alice's defense of the gardeners represents a direct challenge to the Queen's authority. The incident occurs within the broader context of the Queen's croquet game, where her arbitrary execution sentences transform every moment into an exercise in chaos and terror.

== References ==

- Alice's Adventures in Wonderland

[[Category:Événements]]
